lustre.description: Determines whether resources will be created (affects all resources)
lustre.name: create

lustre.description: The ID of the source backup to create the filesystem from
lustre.name: backup_id

lustre.description: Adds support for Elastic Fabric Adapter (EFA) and GPUDirect Storage (GDS) to Lustre. This must be set at creation. If set this cannot be changed and this prevents changes to per_unit_storage_throughput. This is only supported when deployment_type is set to PERSISTENT_2, metadata_configuration is used, and an EFA-enabled security group is attached
lustre.name: efa_enabled

lustre.description: Sets the Lustre version for the file system that you're creating
lustre.name: file_system_type_version

lustre.description: Describes the amount of read and write throughput for each 1 tebibyte of storage, in MB/s/TiB, required for the `PERSISTENT_1` and `PERSISTENT_2` deployment_type
lustre.name: per_unit_storage_throughput

lustre.description: The Lustre root squash configuration used when creating an Amazon FSx for Lustre file system. When enabled, root squash restricts root-level access from clients that try to access your file system as a root user
lustre.name: root_squash_configuration

lustre.description: A list of IDs for the subnets that the file system will be accessible from
lustre.name: subnet_ids

lustre.description: If a KMS Key ARN is set, this key will be used to encrypt the corresponding log group. Please be sure that the KMS Key has an appropriate key policy (https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonCloudWatch/latest/logs/encrypt-log-data-kms.html)
lustre.name: cloudwatch_log_group_kms_key_id

lustre.description: The filesystem deployment type. One of: `SCRATCH_1`, `SCRATCH_2`, `PERSISTENT_1`, `PERSISTENT_2`
lustre.name: deployment_type

lustre.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the file system
lustre.name: timeouts

lustre.description: Number of days to retain log events. Default retention - 90 days
lustre.name: cloudwatch_log_group_retention_in_days

lustre.description: Specifies the ID of the AWS Key Management Service (AWS KMS) key to use for encrypting data on an Amazon File Cache
lustre.name: file_cache_kms_key_id

lustre.description: Determines if a security group is created
lustre.name: create_security_group

lustre.description: Name to use on security group created
lustre.name: security_group_name

lustre.description: Determines whether the security group name (`security_group_name`) is used as a prefix
lustre.name: security_group_use_name_prefix

lustre.description: Description of the security group created
lustre.name: security_group_description

lustre.description: A boolean flag indicating whether tags for the file system should be copied to backups
lustre.name: copy_tags_to_backups

lustre.description: A list of IDs for the security groups that apply to the specified network interfaces created for file system access. These security groups will apply to all network interfaces
lustre.name: security_group_ids

lustre.description: Throughput in MBps required for the `INTELLIGENT_TIERING` storage type. Must be 4000 or multiples of 4000
lustre.name: throughput_capacity

lustre.description: Specified the log class of the log group. Possible values are: `STANDARD` or `INFREQUENT_ACCESS`
lustre.name: cloudwatch_log_group_class

lustre.description: A map of additional tags to add to the cloudwatch log group created
lustre.name: cloudwatch_log_group_tags

lustre.description: The preferred time to take daily automatic backups, in the UTC time zone.
lustre.name: daily_automatic_backup_start_time

lustre.description: The preferred start time (in d:HH:MM format) to perform weekly maintenance, in the UTC time zone
lustre.name: weekly_maintenance_start_time

lustre.description: Determines whether a log group is created by this module for the cluster logs. If not, AWS will automatically create one if logging is enabled
lustre.name: create_cloudwatch_log_group

lustre.description: Whether to create a backup of the file system
lustre.name: create_backup

lustre.description: Create and delete timeout configurations for the backup
lustre.name: backup_timeouts

lustre.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the data repository associations
lustre.name: data_repository_associations_timeouts

lustre.description: A boolean flag indicating whether tags for the cache should be copied to data repository associations. This value defaults to `false`
lustre.name: file_cache_copy_tags_to_data_repository_associations

lustre.description: The configuration object for Amazon FSx for Lustre
lustre.name: file_cache_lustre_configuration

lustre.description: The Lustre data read cache configuration used when creating an Amazon FSx for Lustre file system. Used to configure sizing mode for the cache and size of the file system's SSD read cache, in gibibytes (GiB).
lustre.name: data_read_cache_configuration

lustre.description: The type of drive cache used by `PERSISTENT_1` filesystems that are provisioned with `HDD` `storage_type`
lustre.name: drive_cache_type

lustre.description: The name of the file system
lustre.name: name

lustre.description: The filesystem storage type. One of `SSD`, `HDD` or `INTELLIGENT_TIERING`, defaults to `SSD`. `HDD` is only supported on `PERSISTENT_1` deployment types. `INTELLIGENT_TIERING` requires `data_read_cache_configuration` and `metadata_configuration` to be set and is only supported for `PERSISTENT_2` deployment types
lustre.name: storage_type

lustre.description: Determines whether the log group name should be prefixed with the `cloudwatch_log_group_name` provided
lustre.name: cloudwatch_log_group_use_name_prefix

lustre.description: A map of additional tags to assign to the backup
lustre.name: backup_tags

lustre.description: A map of data repository associations to create
lustre.name: data_repository_associations

lustre.description: Security group ingress rules to add to the security group created
lustre.name: security_group_ingress_rules

lustre.description: ARN for the KMS Key to encrypt the file system at rest. Defaults to an AWS managed KMS Key
lustre.name: kms_key_id

lustre.description: The Lustre metadata configuration used when creating an Amazon FSx for Lustre file system. This can be used to specify a user provisioned metadata scale. This is only supported when deployment_type is set to PERSISTENT_2
lustre.name: metadata_configuration

lustre.description: Determines whether a file cache is created
lustre.name: create_file_cache

lustre.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
lustre.name: tags

lustre.description: Sets the data compression configuration for the file system. Valid values are `LZ4` and `NONE`. Default value is `NONE`
lustre.name: data_compression_type

lustre.description: The configuration object for Amazon FSx for Lustre file systems used in the CreateFileSystem and CreateFileSystemFromBackup operations.
lustre.name: log_configuration

lustre.description: The version for the type of cache that you're creating
lustre.name: file_cache_type_version

lustre.description: The storage capacity of the cache in gibibytes (GiB). Valid values are 1200 GiB, 2400 GiB, and increments of 2400 GiB
lustre.name: file_cache_storage_capacity

lustre.description: Security group egress rules to add to the security group created
lustre.name: security_group_egress_rules

lustre.description: A map of additional tags to add to the security group created
lustre.name: security_group_tags

lustre.description: The number of days to retain automatic backups. Setting this to 0 disables automatic backups. You can retain automatic backups for a maximum of 90 days. only valid for `PERSISTENT_1` and `PERSISTENT_2` deployment_type
lustre.name: automatic_backup_retention_days

lustre.description: The storage capacity (GiB) of the file system
lustre.name: storage_capacity

lustre.description: Name of the CloudWatch Log Group to send logs to. Note: `/aws/fsx/` is pre-pended to the name provided as this is a requirement by FSx
lustre.name: cloudwatch_log_group_name

ontap.description: The ONTAP administrative password for the fsxadmin user that you can use to administer your file system using the ONTAP CLI and REST API
ontap.name: fsx_admin_password

ontap.description: ARN for the KMS Key to encrypt the file system at rest. Defaults to an AWS managed KMS Key
ontap.name: kms_key_id

ontap.description: The filesystem storage type. defaults to `SSD`
ontap.name: storage_type

ontap.description: The number of ha_pairs to deploy for the file system. Valid values are 1 through 6. Recommend only using this parameter for 2 or more ha pairs
ontap.name: ha_pairs

ontap.description: The name of the file system
ontap.name: name

ontap.description: A list of IDs for the security groups that apply to the specified network interfaces created for file system access. These security groups will apply to all network interfaces
ontap.name: security_group_ids

ontap.description: The storage capacity (GiB) of the file system
ontap.name: storage_capacity

ontap.description: Sets the throughput capacity (in MBps) for the file system that you're creating. Valid values are `128`, `256`, `512`, `1024`, `2048`, and `4096`. Either `throughput_capacity` or `throughput_capacity_per_ha_pair` must be specified
ontap.name: throughput_capacity

ontap.description: Specifies the IP address range in which the endpoints to access your file system will be created. By default, Amazon FSx selects an unused IP address range for you from the 198.19.* range
ontap.name: endpoint_ip_address_range

ontap.description: A list of IDs for the subnets that the file system will be accessible from
ontap.name: subnet_ids

ontap.description: Determines whether the security group name (`security_group_name`) is used as a prefix
ontap.name: security_group_use_name_prefix

ontap.description: Description of the security group created
ontap.name: security_group_description

ontap.description: A map of additional tags to add to the security group created
ontap.name: security_group_tags

ontap.description: Determines whether resources will be created (affects all resources)
ontap.name: create

ontap.description: The SSD IOPS configuration for the Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP file system
ontap.name: disk_iops_configuration

ontap.description: The ID for a subnet. A subnet is a range of IP addresses in your virtual private cloud (VPC)
ontap.name: preferred_subnet_id

ontap.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the volumes
ontap.name: volumes_timeouts

ontap.description: Determines if a security group is created
ontap.name: create_security_group

ontap.description: The number of days to retain automatic backups. Setting this to 0 disables automatic backups. You can retain automatic backups for a maximum of 90 days
ontap.name: automatic_backup_retention_days

ontap.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the file system
ontap.name: timeouts

ontap.description: A recurring daily time, in the format `HH:MM`. HH is the zero-padded hour of the day (0-23), and MM is the zero-padded minute of the hour. For example, `05:00` specifies 5 AM daily. Requires `automatic_backup_retention_days` to be set
ontap.name: daily_automatic_backup_start_time

ontap.description: Specifies the VPC route tables in which your file system's endpoints will be created. You should specify all VPC route tables associated with the subnets in which your clients are located. By default, Amazon FSx selects your VPC's default route table
ontap.name: route_table_ids

ontap.description: Name to use on security group created
ontap.name: security_group_name

ontap.description: Security group egress rules to add to the security group created
ontap.name: security_group_egress_rules

ontap.description: A map of tags to add to all resources
ontap.name: tags

ontap.description: Sets the throughput capacity (in MBps) for the file system that you're creating. Valid values are `3072`, `6144`. This parameter should only be used when specifying the `ha_pairs` parameter. Either `throughput_capacity` or `throughput_capacity_per_ha_pair` must be specified
ontap.name: throughput_capacity_per_ha_pair

ontap.description: Create, update, and delete timeout configurations for the storage virtual machines
ontap.name: storage_virtual_machines_timeouts

ontap.description: Security group ingress rules to add to the security group created
ontap.name: security_group_ingress_rules

ontap.description: The filesystem deployment type. One of: `MULTI_AZ_1` or `SINGLE_AZ_1`
ontap.name: deployment_type

ontap.description: The preferred start time (in d:HH:MM format) to perform weekly maintenance, in the UTC time zone
ontap.name: weekly_maintenance_start_time

ontap.name: storage_virtual_machines

windows.description: Set of Elastic Network Interface identifiers from which the file system is accessible. As explained in the [documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/fsx/latest/LustreGuide/mounting-on-premises.html), the first network interface returned is the primary network interface
windows.name: file_system_network_interface_ids

windows.description: For `MULTI_AZ_1` deployment types, use this endpoint when performing administrative tasks on the file system using Amazon FSx Remote PowerShell. For `SINGLE_AZ_1` deployment types, this is the DNS name of the file system
windows.name: file_system_remote_administration_endpoint

windows.description: Name of cloudwatch log group created
windows.name: cloudwatch_log_group_name

windows.description: Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the security group
windows.name: security_group_arn

windows.description: ID of the security group
windows.name: security_group_id

windows.description: Amazon Resource Name of the file system
windows.name: file_system_arn

windows.description: IP address of the primary, or preferred, file server
windows.name: file_system_preferred_file_server_ip

windows.description: Arn of cloudwatch log group created
windows.name: cloudwatch_log_group_arn
